etymology.text: Noun of place from the verb صَنَعَ (ṣanaʕa, “to manufacture”).

body.text: [may be sued, agreeably with analogy, as an inf. n.: and as a n. of place, and of time]. A poet says,

A_in~a AlS~aniyEapa lA takuwnu SaniyEapF
                      
                     
                        Hat~aY yuSaAba bihaA Tariyqu AlmaSonaEi
[which may be rendered
Verily that which is a good deed
considered abstractedly, or without relation to the manner or object &c.,
will not be a good deed
in effect
except,
or
unless, the way of the doing,
or
the way that leads to the place
(here meaning
the object
of the doing, be rightly hit upon therewith
]. (O, TA.) ―         -b2-  In the following verse of Náfi' Ibn-Lakeet, (TA in this art. and in art.
,) wrongly ascribed by J [in arts.
and
] to Lebeed, (TA in art.
,) and ascribed by others to other poets, (TA in art.
,) it is expl. by IAar as signifying
A place that is deemed goodly
[in workmanship]; syn.
[a n. of place, accord. to a general rule, as well as pass. part. n.: or
may be here more literally rendered
a place of skilful workmanship
]: the poet says,

muruTu Alqi*aA*i falayosa fiyhi maSonaEN
                      
                     
                        laA Alr~iy$u yanofaEuhu walaA Alt~aEoqiybu
(TA in the present art.) meaning
Having no feathers upon it,
and having in it no place exhibiting skilful workmanship, neither the feathers being of use to it
nor the binding around with sinews.
(TA in art.
.) ―         -b3-  See also what here follows.
